formulas.name: Aggregate Demand (AD)
formulas.variables: C: Consumption, I: Investment, G: Government Spending, X: Exports, M: Imports
formulas.notes: Used to calculate the total demand for goods and services in an economy

formulas.name: National Income (NI)
formulas.formula: NI = C + I + G + (X - M) - Depreciation
formulas.variables: C: Consumption, I: Investment, G: Government Spending, X: Exports, M: Imports, Depreciation: Wear and Tear of Capital
formulas.notes: Used to calculate the total income earned by the factors of production in an economy

formulas.name: Gross Domestic Product (GDP)
formulas.variables: C: Consumption, I: Investment, G: Government Spending, X: Exports, M: Imports
formulas.notes: Used to calculate the total value of goods and services produced within a country

formulas.name: Marginal Propensity to Consume (MPC)
formulas.variables: ΔC: Change in Consumption, ΔY: Change in Income
formulas.notes: Used to calculate the proportion of additional income that is spent on consumption

formulas.name: Marginal Propensity to Save (MPS)
formulas.variables: ΔS: Change in Saving, ΔY: Change in Income
formulas.notes: Used to calculate the proportion of additional income that is saved

formulas.name: Multiplier (K)
formulas.notes: Used to calculate the effect of an increase in investment on the overall level of income

formulas.name: Investment Multiplier with Government Spending
formulas.variables: MPC: Marginal Propensity to Consume, ΔG: Change in Government Spending
formulas.notes: Used to calculate the effect of an increase in government spending on the overall level of income

formulas.name: Tax Multiplier
formulas.variables: MPC: Marginal Propensity to Consume, ΔT: Change in Tax
formulas.notes: Used to calculate the effect of a change in tax on the overall level of income

formulas.name: Equilibrium Level of Income
formulas.formula: Y = (A + I) / (1 - MPC) or Y = C + I
formulas.variables: Y: Equilibrium Level of Income, A: Autonomous Consumption, I: Investment, MPC: Marginal Propensity to Consume, C: Consumption
formulas.notes: Used to calculate the equilibrium level of income in an economy
